titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/143

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=417 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:08:16.372253+00 | translation moa=418 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:08:19.869488+00

div.p:
Vniuersis has Litteras uisuris uel audituris Magister . W . de Lindesei Decanus Glasguensis Eternam in domino Salutem . Nouerint uni
uersi quod . . Abbas et conuentus de kelkou coram nobis quicquid iuris habebant in terris et pasturis de Molle infra
partem domini Walteri filii Alani Senscalli Scot’ . et etiam communiam quam habebant in Mora de Inuerwic eidem
Waltero et heredibus suis et suis attornatis resignauerunt et inperpetuum quieta clamauerunt . nichil iuris in
eisdem decetero reclamaturi pro quadam parte dicte More de Inuerwic ab eodem . W . eis in escambium collata
per has diuisas . Videlicet a Bothkyl ascendendo per Riuulum qui cadit a scoulande usque ad scoulandesheued ubi
sulcus trahitur . et per illum sulcum usque ad uiam que tendit de Risibrigg’ uersus Inuerwich et sic ab
illa uia ascendendo per sulcum uersus orientem usque ad quandam fossam . et sic ab illa fossa descendendo per sul
cum usque ad Meldrescloesheued . et sic per Riuulum de Meldrescloesheued semper descendendo per maninet . usque
per buccam de Estfulhope . et sic ascendendo per Estfulhope uersus occidentem iuxta diuisas de ellum et de Ri
sibrigg’ usque in Westfulhope . et de Westfulhope usque in Withedre . et de Withedre usque in Bothkil .
et sic semper ascendendo per Bothkil usque in predictam riuulum qui cadit de Scouland’ . Hiis testibus . Domino
. W . dei gratia Glasguensi Episcopo . Scot’ Cancellario . Domino . W . Abbate de Passeleth . Magistro . H . Archidiacono glas
guensi . et multis aliis . e quibus fuerunt . Magister . W . de Grenlau . Magister Abel . et Magister Petrus de Alinton’
qui ad maiorem huius rei securitatem una nobiscum presenti scripto Sigilla sua apposuerunt . Actum apud kelkou
anno gracie . . .

To all who shall see or hear these letters, Master William of Lindsay, dean of Glasgow, wishes eternal salvation in the Lord. Let all people know that Abbot [Herbert] and the monastic community of Kelso have in our presence resigned and quitclaimed for ever any right they used to have in the lands and pastures of Mow within the portion belonging to the lord Walter son of Alan, Steward of Scotland, and also the right of common that they used to have in the moor of Innerwick to the same Walter and his heirs and their attorneys; no right in the same is to be reclaimed in return for a portion of the said moor of Innerwick from the same William in the exchange bestowed by these boundaries, namely, from Birk Cleugh Bothwell going up by the small stream which falls from Scullins as far as Scullins Head where the ditch is drawn, and by that ditch as far as the road which stretches from Rise Lea towards Innerwick, and so from that road going up by the ditch eastwards as far as a certain furrow, and so from the furrow going down by the ditch as far as ‘Meldrescloesheued’, and so by the little stream of ‘Meldrescloesheued’ continually going down by Monynut, as far as the edge of Easter Wide Hope, and so going up by Easter Wide Hope westwards next to the bounds of ‘Ellum’ and of Rise Lea, as far as Wester Wide Hope, and from Wester Wide Hope as far as Whiteadder, and from Whiteadder far as Birk Cleugh, and so continually going down by Birk Cleugh as far as the said little stream which falls from Scullins. With these witnesses: the lord William, by the grace of God, bishop of Glasgow, Chancellor of Scotland; the lord William, abbot of Paisley; Master Hugh, archdeacon of Glasgow; and many others, among whom there were Master William of Greenlaw, Master Abel, and Master Peter of Alinton, who for the greater security of thie matter together with us have applied their own seal to the present written deed. Done at Kelso in the year of grace . . .
